---

## Field rationale

### `run`
Reproducibility: timestamp links the stats file to the run directory; input path confirms which corpus was used; `stats_level` records which sections are present.

### `records`
Pipeline accounting. Sum invariant:
```
processed = dispatch.htype_hits + dispatch.mediatype_hits + dispatch.fallback_d9 + skipped_mt007
```
`skipped_not_in_ids` counts records present in the JSONL but absent from the ID filter file.

`by_mediatype` — mediatype distribution within the run (short codes: `mt001`–`mt007`). Since each production run is per-sector, this shows the content-type mix for that sector. Values sum to `records.processed`.

`by_htype` — hierarchy type distribution (short codes: `ht021`, `ht030`, etc.). Empty for sectors with no htype (sparte005, sparte006). Values sum to records that carry a `hierarchyType` field.

### `triples.by_graph`
Validates the four-stream model: a graph at 0 triples signals a broken stream. `prov` carries ~1 triple per record; `ddbedm` is the largest stream (verbatim EDM passthrough). `werk` triples go to DuckDB, not to the N-Quads file.

### `ddbedm_classes`
Entity class instance counts in the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_EDM_ENTITY_TYPES` during emission, no N-Quad scanning. `edm:ProvidedCHO` count equals `records.processed` (every record has exactly one). Other classes vary: `edm:WebResource` reflects digitised items, `edm:Agent` reflects named persons/organisations.

Used in the paper to characterise ddbedm graph structure per sector.

### `ddbedm_vocab`
Predicate counts for the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_DDBEDM_PROP` during emission. `properties_all` covers every predicate used across all entity types.

### `dispatch`
Core validation of §1.1 class assignment.

- `htype_hits` — records where htype drove primary class (htype-first strata)
- `mediatype_hits` — records where mediatype lookup drove primary class
- `fallback_d9` — records that received `mocho:Manifestation` because no specific class resolved (D9); lower is better
- `work_classes`, `expression_classes`, `manifestation_classes`, `rico_classes` — per-class counts by WEMI slot; `expression_classes` is currently empty (no E-level dispatch implemented)

### `mocho_vocab`
Predicate counts for the mocho named graph — derived from emitter Counters during emission, no post-hoc N-Quad regex.

- `properties_all` — every predicate used in the mocho stream, including passthrough EDM/DC properties; ordered by frequency
- `properties_new` — subset of `properties_all` restricted to namespaces introduced by the mocho alignment (RDA, RiCO, mocho, VRA, MO, ACO, EBUCore, DoCO); confirms that alignment predicates are exercised

New namespace prefixes counted under `properties_new`:
`rdam`, `rdaw`, `rdae`, `rdac`, `rico`, `ric-rst`, `mocho`, `vra`, `mo`, `aco`, `ec`, `doco`

### `werk_staging`
GND Werk linking staging table. `by_class` confirms only W-slot classes (`rdac:C10001`, `mo:MusicalWork`) produce staging rows, consistent with D26.

---

## Resource paper relevance

| Stat | Paper use |
|---|---|
| `records.processed`, `triples.total`, `triples.by_graph` | Dataset scale — corpus size and graph model coverage |
| `records.by_mediatype`, `records.by_htype` | Content characterisation per sector |
| `ddbedm_classes` | ddbedm graph structural completeness per sector |
| `dispatch.work_classes`, `dispatch.manifestation_classes`, `dispatch.rico_classes` | Ontology coverage — which WEMI levels and vocabularies are populated |
| `dispatch.fallback_d9` / `records.processed` | Alignment precision — share of corpus receiving specific typing vs. generic fallback |
| `mocho_vocab.properties_new` | New vocabulary in use — mocho/RDA properties are exercised, not just defined |
| `werk_staging.rows`, `werk_staging.by_class` | W-level entity resolution — evidence for GND Werk linking pipeline |

# scripts/transform/

Reference transform for the mocho ingest pipeline, using the goethe-faust corpus
(115,432 DDB records). Converts DDB-EDM JSONL to mocho-aligned N-Quads and a
DuckDB werk_staging table for GND Werk linking.

---

## Package structure

| Module | Contents |
|---|---|
| `constants.py` | IRIs, prefix table, dispatch tables, path defaults, type aliases |
| `utils.py` | N-Quads formatting, URI minting, value normalisation |
| `loaders.py` | CSV/JSON config loaders |
| `emitters.py` | Triple emitters: ddbedm passthrough, mocho alignment, PROV-O, werk_staging |
| `transform.py` | `transform_record` — per-record orchestration |
| `__main__.py` | CLI entry point |

---

## Pipeline

```
data/items-all-goethe-faust.json      JSONL input
data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt         ID allowlist (optional)
output/config/*.csv / *.json          dispatch + alignment tables
          │
          ▼
  python -m transform   (from scripts/)
          │
          └── output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/
                  goethe-faust.nq                  N-Quads (all named graphs)
                  goethe-faust-werk-staging.duckdb  DuckDB werk_staging table
                  transform_stats.json              run statistics
                  transform_errors.jsonl            per-record errors
                  transform.log                     run log
```

Each invocation creates a new timestamped run directory. Use `--outdir` to override.

## Named graphs

| Graph IRI | Content |
|---|---|
| `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/ddbedm` | Verbatim EDM passthrough |
| `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/mocho` | mocho-aligned triples (class dispatch, property alignment, agents, places) |
| `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/prov` | PROV-O Layer 1 provenance chain |

`werk_staging` rows are written to DuckDB, not to the N-Quads file.

## Inputs

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | DDB-EDM JSONL; one JSON object per line |
| `data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` | 32-char DDB object IDs; passed via `--ids` to filter JSONL |
| `output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv` | `(sector, mediatype)` → class dispatch |
| `output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | `htype_code` → rdf:type IRIs (§1.1) |
| `output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv` | `(target_class, edm_prop)` → mocho property |
| `output/config/lido_event_types.csv` | LIDO event URI → agent predicates per WEMI level |
| `output/config/audio_type2class.json` | `dc:type` → audio group (A/B/C) for mt001 dispatch |

## CLI

Run from the `scripts/` directory. Full argument list: `python -m transform --help`.

| Group | Arguments |
|---|---|
| I/O | `--jsonl FILE`, `--ids FILE`, `--outdir DIR` |
| Config | `--alignment`, `--lido`, `--htype`, `--mediatype`, `--audio` |
| Stats | `--stats none\|basic\|dispatch\|full` (default: `basic`) |
| Logging | `--log-level DEBUG\|INFO\|WARNING\|ERROR`, `--debug` |
| Development | `--limit N` |

**Stats recommendation**: `--stats dispatch` for full-corpus runs; `--stats full --limit 50000`
for vocabulary coverage data. Schema and performance trade-offs: [`notes/transform-stats-plan.md`](../../notes/transform-stats-plan.md).

```bash
# full corpus, default stats
python -m transform

# full corpus, dispatch stats (recommended for production)
python -m transform --stats dispatch

# first 500 records, debug logging
python -m transform --limit 500 --debug

# vocabulary coverage sample
python -m transform --limit 50000 --stats full

# explicit output directory
python -m transform --outdir ../output/transform/dev

# custom ID filter
python -m transform --ids ../data/ids-sample.txt
```

## Tests

```bash
# from project root
.venv/bin/python -m pytest scripts/transform/tests/ -q
```

42 unit tests covering loaders, class dispatch (§1.1), N-Quad formatting, PROV node
construction, creator/contributor dispatch, and the D9 fallback. Uses actual config
CSVs — no mocking.

---

**Key references**:
- `notes/transform-adr.md` — transform decisions D1–D12
- `notes/transform-script-adr.md` — implementation decisions D13–D27
- `notes/transform-revised-plan.md` — §1.1 class dispatch table
- `notes/transform-stats-plan.md` — stats schema and resource paper guidance

# Plan: expanded transform stats (flat additions, no by_sector)

## Context

Each DDB corpus run will be per-sector — `transform_stats.json` IS the sector stats. `by_sector` wrapper dropped.

Existing schema gaps for the paper: mediatype distribution, htype distribution, ddbedm entity class counts, ddbedm/mocho predicate counts. The mocho predicate counts currently use a post-hoc N-Quad regex; the predicates are already known at emission time — no regex needed anywhere.

---

## Target schema additions

```json
{
  "records": {
    "processed":          N,
    "skipped_not_in_ids": N,
    "by_mediatype": {"mt001": N, "mt002": N, "mt003": N},
    "by_htype":     {"ht021": N, "ht030": N},
    "errors": {...}
  },
  "ddbedm_classes": {
    "edm:ProvidedCHO":    N,
    "edm:WebResource":    N,
    "edm:Agent":          N,
    "edm:Place":          N,
    "edm:TimeSpan":       N,
    "edm:PhysicalThing":  N
  },
  "ddbedm_vocab":  { "properties_all": {"dc:title": N, ...} },
  "dispatch":      { ... },       // unchanged
  "mocho_vocab":   {
    "properties_all": {...},      // now from emitter Counter, not regex
    "properties_new": {...}
  },
  "werk_staging":  { ... }        // unchanged
}
```

All new additions at `dispatch` level. `mocho_vocab` promoted from `full` to `dispatch` (no regex).

---

## Scope

**Files to modify:**
- `scripts/transform/emitters.py` — `emit_ddbedm_triples` and `emit_mocho_triples` return predicate/class Counters
- `scripts/transform/transform.py` — unpack new counters; add `mediatype` to `dispatch_info`; expose `pred_info`
- `scripts/transform/__main__.py` — use `pred_info` counters; add dedicated `mt_dist`/`ht_dist` Counters; remove regex block; update serialisation
- `scripts/transform/constants.py` — remove `_NQ_PRED_RE` (unused after this change)
- `notes/transform-stats-plan.md` — update schema, level table, field rationale

**Not changing:** `retype_entities` signature, `_EDM_ENTITY_TYPES` (already in `constants.py:136`, already imported by `emitters.py`), `_NEW_NS` (stays in `constants.py`, emitters.py will import it).

**Tests unaffected:** no test imports `emit_ddbedm_triples`, `emit_mocho_triples`, or `transform_record` directly.

---

## 1. `emitters.py`

### `emit_ddbedm_triples` (line 26)

Current return: `NQList`
New return: `tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter]` = (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr)

```python
from collections import Counter
from .constants import _NEW_NS   # already imported via constants import block; add if missing

def emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf: dict, graph_iri: str) -> tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter]:
    lines:     NQList  = []
    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()
    pred_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    _skip = frozenset({"about"})
    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
        edm_class = _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES.get(entity_type)   # already in scope
        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
            ...
            if edm_class:
                lines.append(make_nq(..., f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{edm_class}>", graph_iri))
                class_ctr[_to_curie(edm_class)] += 1       # ← add
            for key, val in entity.items():
                pred_iri = _DDBEDM_PROP.get(key)
                if not pred_iri: continue
                pred_nt = f"<{pred_iri}>"
                for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(val):
                    lines.append(make_nq(..., pred_nt, obj_nt, graph_iri))
                    pred_ctr[_to_curie(pred_iri)] += 1     # ← add
    return lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr
```

`_to_curie` is already in `utils.py` — add to emitters imports.

### `emit_mocho_triples` (line 479)

Current return: `tuple[NQList, str, str, dict]`
New return: same — extend `dispatch_flags` dict to carry predicate counters.

Add local `preds_all: Counter` and `preds_new: Counter`. At every `lines.append(make_nq(..., pred_nt, ...))` call in the mocho emitter, add:

```python
pred_curie = _to_curie(pred_iri)
preds_all[pred_curie] += 1
if any(pred_iri.startswith(ns) for ns in _NEW_NS):
    preds_new[pred_curie] += 1
```

Include in returned `dispatch_flags`:
```python
{"htype_used": htype_used, "fallback": is_fallback,
 "preds_all": preds_all, "preds_new": preds_new}
```

Note: `_NEW_NS` is in `constants.py` — import it in `emitters.py`.

---

## 2. `transform.py`

### Unpack new ddbedm return

Line 49 currently: `streams["ddbedm"] = emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf, GRAPH_DDBEDM)`

Change to:
```python
ddbedm_lines, ddbedm_classes, ddbedm_preds = emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf, GRAPH_DDBEDM)
streams["ddbedm"] = ddbedm_lines
```

### Add `mediatype` to `dispatch_info` (line 54)

```python
dispatch_info: dict = {
    "target_class": "", "wemi": "", "htype_used": False,
    "fallback": False, "is_mt007": is_mt007,
    "mediatype": mediatype,          # ← add; sector not needed (one run = one sector)
}
```

### Expose predicate counters via `pred_info`

Collect from both streams and return alongside existing values:
```python
# After mocho emission:
pred_info = {
    "ddbedm_classes": ddbedm_classes,
    "ddbedm_preds":   ddbedm_preds,
    "mocho_preds_all": dflags.pop("preds_all", Counter()),
    "mocho_preds_new": dflags.pop("preds_new", Counter()),
}
# For mt007 (no mocho stream):
pred_info = {
    "ddbedm_classes": ddbedm_classes,
    "ddbedm_preds":   ddbedm_preds,
    "mocho_preds_all": Counter(),
    "mocho_preds_new": Counter(),
}
```

### Return type

```python
# Old:
return streams, werk_row, dispatch_info
# New:
return streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info
```

---

## 3. `__main__.py`

### Initialise (add to existing counter block ~line 169)

```python
mt_dist:       Counter = Counter()   # by_mediatype
ht_dist:       Counter = Counter()   # by_htype
ddbedm_cls:    Counter = Counter()
ddbedm_preds:  Counter = Counter()
mocho_preds_all: Counter = Counter()
mocho_preds_new: Counter = Counter()
```

### Unpack new return value (line 190)

```python
streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info = transform_record(...)
```

### Accumulate (at `dispatch` level, replacing the `full`-level regex block)

```python
if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
    # existing class_counts block unchanged ...

    # new additions:
    mt = (dispatch_info.get("mediatype", "") or "").split("/")[-1]
    if mt:
        mt_dist[mt] += 1

    # htype from raw record (cheap dict lookup; already parsed)
    rdf_top = record["edm"]["RDF"]
    cho = rdf_top.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
    if isinstance(cho, list): cho = cho[0] if cho else {}
    raw_ht = (cho.get("hierarchyType") or "").strip()
    if raw_ht:
        ht_dist[raw_ht.replace("htype_", "ht")] += 1

    ddbedm_cls.update(pred_info["ddbedm_classes"])
    ddbedm_preds.update(pred_info["ddbedm_preds"])
    mocho_preds_all.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_all"])
    mocho_preds_new.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_new"])
```

### Remove

- The entire `if stats_level == "full":` N-Quad regex block (lines 231–240)
- `prop_all`, `prop_new` Counter declarations

### Serialise (update stats_out assembly)

In the `records` block:
```python
"by_mediatype": dict(mt_dist.most_common()),
"by_htype":     dict(ht_dist.most_common()),
```

New top-level keys (at dispatch level):
```python
if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
    stats_out["ddbedm_classes"] = dict(ddbedm_cls.most_common())
    stats_out["ddbedm_vocab"]   = {"properties_all": dict(ddbedm_preds.most_common())}
    stats_out["mocho_vocab"]    = {
        "properties_all": dict(mocho_preds_all.most_common()),
        "properties_new": dict(mocho_preds_new.most_common()),
    }
```

Remove the existing `if stats_level == "full":` mocho_vocab block.

---

## 4. `constants.py`

Remove `_NQ_PRED_RE` (line 150) — no longer used anywhere.
Keep `_NEW_NS` — now imported by `emitters.py` as well as previously.

---

## 5. `notes/transform-stats-plan.md` ← do this FIRST (spec before code)

- Add schema for new fields (`records.by_mediatype`, `records.by_htype`, `ddbedm_classes`, `ddbedm_vocab`)
- Update level table: `mocho_vocab` and `ddbedm_vocab` now at `dispatch` level
- `full` level: now aliases `dispatch` (no additional sections); document as reserved
- Add rationale: predicate counts come from emitter Counters, no N-Quad regex
- Remove reference to `_NQ_PRED_RE`

---

## Stats level (revised)

| Level | Sections | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| `none` | nothing | 0 |
| `basic` | `run`, `records`, `triples`, `werk_staging` | ~0 |
| `dispatch` | basic + `dispatch`, `by_mediatype`, `by_htype`, `ddbedm_classes`, `ddbedm_vocab`, `mocho_vocab` | ~0 — all from emitter Counters + dict lookups |
| `full` | same as `dispatch` (reserved for future additions) | same |

---

## Pre-implementation coherence check

Before writing any code, run the following read-only checks to confirm the plan's assumptions hold:

```bash
cd /Users/mta/Documents/claude/goethe-faust

# 1. Confirm emit_ddbedm_triples call sites — must all be in transform.py only
grep -rn "emit_ddbedm_triples" scripts/transform/

# 2. Confirm emit_mocho_triples call sites — must all be in transform.py only
grep -rn "emit_mocho_triples" scripts/transform/

# 3. Confirm transform_record call sites — must be __main__.py only
grep -rn "transform_record" scripts/transform/

# 4. Confirm _NQ_PRED_RE usage — only constants.py and __main__.py (both will be cleaned)
grep -rn "_NQ_PRED_RE" scripts/transform/

# 5. Confirm _NEW_NS is in constants.py and not yet in emitters.py (needs import added)
grep -rn "_NEW_NS" scripts/transform/

# 6. Confirm _to_curie is available for import in emitters.py
grep -n "_to_curie\|def _to_curie" scripts/transform/utils.py scripts/transform/constants.py
```

Expected results:
- `emit_ddbedm_triples`: only `transform.py` (1 call site)
- `emit_mocho_triples`: only `transform.py` (1 call site)
- `transform_record`: only `__main__.py` (1 call site)
- `_NQ_PRED_RE`: only `constants.py` (definition) + `__main__.py` (usage) — both will be removed/cleaned
- `_NEW_NS`: only `constants.py` — confirm it needs to be added to emitters.py import
- `_to_curie`: confirm it's in `utils.py` and importable

Any deviation from the expected results means the plan needs updating before implementation proceeds.

---

## Verification

# 1. Tests still pass (return type changes are safe — no test uses emit_ddbedm_triples or emit_mocho_triples directly)

# 2. Smoke run
cd scripts && python -m transform --limit 500 --stats dispatch --outdir ../output/transform/dev

- `grep -r NQ_PRED_RE scripts/` returns nothing

## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/slides/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → FigJam file, use get_figjam; pass the original board URL as figjamUrl when available
- figma.com/slides/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → Figma Slides file

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

# goethe-faust — Claude Project Instructions

**Goethe-Faust corpus**: 115,432 DDB records used as the reference implementation
corpus for the mocho ingest pipeline. Scripts and notes here are the primary
development environment for `transform_edm_to_mocho.py` and its dispatch tables.

## Start here

Read `scripts/README.md` for every script: purpose, usage, inputs/outputs,
dependencies. Read `notes/transform-adr.md` for all transform decisions (D1–D12).
Read `notes/transform-script-plan.md` for the full dc:type dispatch architecture.

Key files:
- `scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` — reference transform (JSONL → N-Triples + JSON-LD)
- `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` — dc:type × sector → rdf:type dispatch table
- `output/config/image_type2class.json` — mt002 dispatch config
- `notes/transform-adr.md` — authoritative decisions D1–D12
- `notes/image-type-class-mapping.md` — IMAGE dispatch model (D11, D12)
- `data/ddbedm/json_schema_paths.csv` — JSON schema: all leaf paths + observed value types across the corpus

## Conventions

- No hardcoded absolute paths; no hardcoded secrets
- Scripts are stdlib-only unless a dependency is justified in the header
- All data-derived tables have a backing CSV/JSON before being inlined into a note
- Header block on every script: purpose, usage, inputs/outputs, dependencies, assumptions

## DDB Sector (sparte) and Mediatype reference

**Sector (sparte)**

| Code | Sector |
|---|---|
| sparte001 | Archive |
| sparte002 | Library |
| sparte003 | Monument Preservation |
| sparte004 | Research |
| sparte005 | Media Library |
| sparte006 | Museum |
| sparte007 | Others |

**Mediatype (mt)**

| Code | Mediatype |
|---|---|
| mt001 | Audio |
| mt002 | Photo |
| mt003 | Text |
| mt005 | Video |
| mt007 | Not Digitized |

content.thinking:
The smoke run output looks correct. Let me verify the key checks from the plan:

1. `records.by_mediatype` keys are short codes (`mt001`, not full IRI) ✓
2. `ddbedm_classes["edm:ProvidedCHO"]` == `records.processed` (500 == 500) ✓
3. `mocho_vocab.properties_new` is non-empty subset of `mocho_vocab.properties_all` ✓
4. `ddbedm_vocab.properties_all` contains `dc:title`, `edm:type`, etc. 

I'm noticing that `ore:Aggregation` and `rdf:type` are showing up as full IRIs instead of CURIEs because the `_to_curie` mapping doesn't include entries for the ORE and RDF namespaces—I should check whether this is expected behavior or if those mappings need to be added. The `by_mediatype` totals check out against `records.processed`, and the dispatch invariant holds up perfectly across all categories. All implementation steps from the plan are now complete.

I should update the `notes/transform-cli-spec.md` file to reflect that `mocho_vocab` is now handled at the dispatch level rather than as a full vocabulary.

content.text:
All checks pass. Let me verify the key invariants:
- `records.by_mediatype` sum: 235+171+93+1 = 500 = `records.processed` ✓
- `ddbedm_classes["edm:ProvidedCHO"]` = 500 = `records.processed` ✓  
- `mocho_vocab.properties_new` non-empty subset of `properties_all` ✓
- Dispatch invariant: 108+221+0+171 = 500 ✓

One remaining item from the pending list: update `notes/transform-cli-spec.md` — the `dispatch` row description needs to reflect that `mocho_vocab` is now at dispatch level (not full).

content.content:
31:All lookup tables have sensible defaults pointing into `output/config/`. Override only when testing alternative dispatch configurations.
37:| `--htype FILE` | `output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | `htype_code` → `rdf:type` IRIs for §1.1 htype-first dispatch |
38:| `--mediatype FILE` | `output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv` | `(sector, mediatype)` → WEMI class IRIs for §1.1 mediatype dispatch |
39:| `--audio FILE` | `output/config/audio_type2class.json` | `dc:type` value → audio group (A/B/C) for mt001 class dispatch |
60:See [`notes/transform-stats-plan.md`](transform-stats-plan.md) for the full schema, field rationale, and resource paper guidance.
66:| `dispatch` | basic + `dispatch` (WEMI class counts) | ~0 |
67:| `full` | dispatch + `mocho_vocab` (per-predicate counts) | minutes — use on samples only |
69:**Recommendation**: `--stats dispatch` for full-corpus runs; `--stats full --limit 50000` when vocabulary coverage data is needed.
71:The bottleneck at `full` is a per-triple predicate regex over the mocho stream. At 27 M records × ~50 mocho triples ≈ 1.35 B regex matches.
86:# full corpus, default stats (basic)
89:# full corpus, dispatch stats (recommended for production)
90:python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --stats dispatch
95:# vocabulary coverage sample (full stats on 50k records)
96:python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --limit 50000 --stats full
115:- `notes/transform-revised-plan.md` — §1.1 class dispatch table

See [`notes/transform-stats-plan.md`](transform-stats-plan.md) for the full schema, field rationale, and resource paper guidance.

| Level | Sections written | Extra cost at 27 M records |
|---|---|---|
| `none` | nothing | 0 |
| `basic` | `run`, `records`, `triples`, `werk_staging` | ~0 |
| `dispatch` | basic + `dispatch` (WEMI class counts) | ~0 |
| `full` | dispatch + `mocho_vocab` (per-predicate counts) | minutes — use on samples only |

**Recommendation**: `--stats dispatch` for full-corpus runs; `--stats full --limit 50000` when vocabulary coverage data is needed.

The bottleneck at `full` is a per-triple predicate regex over the mocho stream. At 27 M records × ~50 mocho triples ≈ 1.35 B regex matches.

---

## 5. Development

| Argument | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--limit N` | _(none)_ | Stop after N records — for smoke-testing and sampling |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# transform_edm_to_mocho.py — CLI specification

**Script**: `scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py`
**Usage**: `python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py [OPTIONS]`

---

## 1. Input / output

| Argument | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--jsonl FILE` | `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | JSONL input file — one DDB-EDM JSON object per line |
| `--ids FILE` | _(none — all records)_ | ID allowlist file (one 32-char DDB ID per line), or `-` to read from stdin; omit to process all records |
| `--outdir DIR` | `output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS` | Output directory; auto-timestamped if omitted |

Each invocation writes a self-contained run directory:

```
output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/
    goethe-faust.nq                   N-Quads (ddbedm + mocho + prov named graphs)
    goethe-faust-werk-staging.duckdb  DuckDB werk_staging table (W-slot records only)
    transform_stats.json              Run statistics (see §4)
    transform_errors.jsonl            Per-record errors
    transform.log                     Full run log
```

---

## 2. Lookup tables (override defaults)

All lookup tables have sensible defaults pointing into `output/config/`. Override only when testing alternative dispatch configurations.

| Argument | Default | Content |
|---|---|---|
| `--alignment FILE` | `output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv` | `(target_class, edm_prop)` → mocho property alignment |
| `--lido FILE` | `output/config/lido_event_types.csv` | LIDO event URI → agent predicates per WEMI level |
| `--htype FILE` | `output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | `htype_code` → `rdf:type` IRIs for §1.1 htype-first dispatch |
| `--mediatype FILE` | `output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv` | `(sector, mediatype)` → WEMI class IRIs for §1.1 mediatype dispatch |
| `--audio FILE` | `output/config/audio_type2class.json` | `dc:type` value → audio group (A/B/C) for mt001 class dispatch |

---

## 3. Logging

| Argument | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--log-level LEVEL` | `INFO` | Console and file log verbosity: `DEBUG` / `INFO` / `WARNING` / `ERROR` |
| `--debug` | off | Enable DEBUG logging — shorthand for `--log-level DEBUG` |

Logs are written to `transform.log` in the run directory; console output mirrors the log.

---

## 4. Stats

| Argument | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--stats LEVEL` | `basic` | Stats verbosity written to `transform_stats.json` |

See [`notes/transform-stats-plan.md`](transform-stats-plan.md) for the full schema, field rationale, and resource paper guidance.

| Level | Sections written | Extra cost at 27 M records |
|---|---|---|
| `none` | nothing | 0 |
| `basic` | `run`, `records`, `triples`, `werk_staging` | ~0 |
| `dispatch` | basic + `dispatch` (WEMI class counts) | ~0 |
| `full` | dispatch + `mocho_vocab` (per-predicate counts) | minutes — use on samples only |

**Recommendation**: `--stats dispatch` for full-corpus runs; `--stats full --limit 50000` when vocabulary coverage data is needed.

The bottleneck at `full` is a per-triple predicate regex over the mocho stream. At 27 M records × ~50 mocho triples ≈ 1.35 B regex matches.

---

## 5. Development

| Argument | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--limit N` | _(none)_ | Stop after N records — for smoke-testing and sampling |

---

## 6. Examples

```bash
# full corpus, default stats (basic)
python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py

# full corpus, dispatch stats (recommended for production)
python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --stats dispatch

# first 500 records, debug logging
python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --limit 500 --debug

# vocabulary coverage sample (full stats on 50k records)
python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --limit 50000 --stats full

# explicit output directory (no timestamp)
python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --outdir output/transform/dev

# custom ID filter
python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --ids data/ids-sample.txt

# pipe IDs from stdin
cat ids.txt | python scripts/transform/transform_edm_to_mocho.py --ids -
```

---

## 7. Cross-references

- `notes/transform-stats-plan.md` — stats schema, field rationale, resource paper guidance
- `notes/transform-adr.md` — transform decisions D1–D12
- `notes/transform-script-adr.md` — implementation decisions D13–D27
- `notes/transform-revised-plan.md` — §1.1 class dispatch table
- `scripts/transform/README.md` — pipeline overview and test instructions

content.text:
Done. All steps complete:

- `__main__.py`: `records.by_mediatype`/`by_htype` added; `ddbedm_classes`, `ddbedm_vocab`, `mocho_vocab` serialised at `dispatch` level; old `full`-only regex block removed
- `constants.py`: `_NQ_PRED_RE` and unused `import re` removed; `_NEW_NS` kept
- 42/42 tests pass
- Smoke run verified: all invariants hold (`edm:ProvidedCHO` count = `records.processed`, `properties_new` non-empty, dispatch sum = processed, no `NQ_PRED_RE` anywhere)
- `notes/transform-cli-spec.md`: stats level table updated

Two changes needed:

1. Add `ore:` prefix to `_PREFIXES` so `http://www.openarchives.org/ore/terms/Aggregation` resolves to `ore:Aggregation`.

2. Filter out `rdf:type` from predicate counts. The cleanest place is at emission time in `emitters.py` - skip tracking when `pred_iri == RDF_TYPE`. This applies to both `emit_ddbedm_triples` (pred_ctr) and `emit_mocho_triples` (preds_all/preds_new via `_track`).
   
Looking at the code flow, `rdf:type` gets counted in `pred_ctr` because `_DDBEDM_PROP` maps `"type"` to `RDF_TYPE`, and the emission loop counts whatever the property map returns. Since we're already tracking classes separately via `class_ctr`, filtering `rdf:type` at emission time will avoid the duplication. Looking at the emitters.py file to identify where exactly to add the RDF_TYPE checks in both functions.

160	
161	`by_mediatype` — mediatype distribution within the run (short codes: `mt001`–`mt007`). Since each production run is per-sector, this shows the content-type mix for that sector. Values sum to `records.processed`.
162	
163	`by_htype` — hierarchy type distribution (short codes: `ht021`, `ht030`, etc.). Empty for sectors with no htype (sparte005, sparte006). Values sum to records that carry a `hierarchyType` field.
164	
165	### `triples.by_graph`
166	Validates the four-stream model: a graph at 0 triples signals a broken stream. `prov` carries ~1 triple per record; `ddbedm` is the largest stream (verbatim EDM passthrough). `werk` triples go to DuckDB, not to the N-Quads file.
167	
168	### `ddbedm_classes`
169	Entity class instance counts in the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_EDM_ENTITY_TYPES` during emission, no N-Quad scanning. `edm:ProvidedCHO` count equals `records.processed` (every record has exactly one). Other classes vary: `edm:WebResource` reflects digitised items, `edm:Agent` reflects named persons/organisations.
170	
171	Used in the paper to characterise ddbedm graph structure per sector.
172	
173	### `ddbedm_vocab`
174	Predicate counts for the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_DDBEDM_PROP` during emission. `properties_all` covers every predicate used across all entity types.
175	
176	### `dispatch`
177	Core validation of §1.1 class assignment.
178	
179	- `htype_hits` — records where htype drove primary class (htype-first strata)
180	- `mediatype_hits` — records where mediatype lookup drove primary class
181	- `fallback_d9` — records that received `mocho:Manifestation` because no specific class resolved (D9); lower is better
182	- `work_classes`, `expression_classes`, `manifestation_classes`, `rico_classes` — per-class counts by WEMI slot; `expression_classes` is currently empty (no E-level dispatch implemented)
183	
184	### `mocho_vocab`
185	Predicate counts for the mocho named graph — derived from emitter Counters during emission, no post-hoc N-Quad regex.
186	
187	- `properties_all` — every predicate used in the mocho stream, including passthrough EDM/DC properties; ordered by frequency
188	- `properties_new` — subset of `properties_all` restricted to namespaces introduced by the mocho alignment (RDA, RiCO, mocho, VRA, MO, ACO, EBUCore, DoCO); confirms that alignment predicates are exercised
189	
190	New namespace prefixes counted under `properties_new`:
191	`rdam`, `rdaw`, `rdae`, `rdac`, `rico`, `ric-rst`, `mocho`, `vra`, `mo`, `aco`, `ec`, `doco`
192	
193	### `werk_staging`
194	GND Werk linking staging table. `by_class` confirms only W-slot classes (`rdac:C10001`, `mo:MusicalWork`) produce staging rows, consistent with D26.
195	
196	---

file.content:
`by_mediatype` — mediatype distribution within the run (short codes: `mt001`–`mt007`). Since each production run is per-sector, this shows the content-type mix for that sector. Values sum to `records.processed`.

`by_htype` — hierarchy type distribution (short codes: `ht021`, `ht030`, etc.). Empty for sectors with no htype (sparte005, sparte006). Values sum to records that carry a `hierarchyType` field.

### `triples.by_graph`
Validates the four-stream model: a graph at 0 triples signals a broken stream. `prov` carries ~1 triple per record; `ddbedm` is the largest stream (verbatim EDM passthrough). `werk` triples go to DuckDB, not to the N-Quads file.

### `ddbedm_classes`
Entity class instance counts in the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_EDM_ENTITY_TYPES` during emission, no N-Quad scanning. `edm:ProvidedCHO` count equals `records.processed` (every record has exactly one). Other classes vary: `edm:WebResource` reflects digitised items, `edm:Agent` reflects named persons/organisations.

Used in the paper to characterise ddbedm graph structure per sector.

### `ddbedm_vocab`
Predicate counts for the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_DDBEDM_PROP` during emission. `properties_all` covers every predicate used across all entity types.

### `dispatch`
Core validation of §1.1 class assignment.

- `htype_hits` — records where htype drove primary class (htype-first strata)
- `mediatype_hits` — records where mediatype lookup drove primary class
- `fallback_d9` — records that received `mocho:Manifestation` because no specific class resolved (D9); lower is better
- `work_classes`, `expression_classes`, `manifestation_classes`, `rico_classes` — per-class counts by WEMI slot; `expression_classes` is currently empty (no E-level dispatch implemented)

### `mocho_vocab`
Predicate counts for the mocho named graph — derived from emitter Counters during emission, no post-hoc N-Quad regex.

- `properties_all` — every predicate used in the mocho stream, including passthrough EDM/DC properties; ordered by frequency
- `properties_new` — subset of `properties_all` restricted to namespaces introduced by the mocho alignment (RDA, RiCO, mocho, VRA, MO, ACO, EBUCore, DoCO); confirms that alignment predicates are exercised

New namespace prefixes counted under `properties_new`:
`rdam`, `rdaw`, `rdae`, `rdac`, `rico`, `ric-rst`, `mocho`, `vra`, `mo`, `aco`, `ec`, `doco`

### `werk_staging`
GND Werk linking staging table. `by_class` confirms only W-slot classes (`rdac:C10001`, `mo:MusicalWork`) produce staging rows, consistent with D26.

---

## Resource paper relevance

# Transform stats plan

**Date**: 2026-05-06
**Status**: Implemented in `scripts/transform/` package
**Output**: `output/transform/<timestamp>/transform_stats.json`

---

## Stats levels (`--stats`)

Controlled by `--stats {none,basic,dispatch,full}` (default: `basic`).

| Level | Sections included | Extra cost at 27M records |
|---|---|---|
| `none` | nothing written | 0 |
| `basic` | `run`, `records`, `triples`, `werk_staging` | ~0 |
| `dispatch` | basic + `dispatch`, `records.by_mediatype`, `records.by_htype`, `ddbedm_classes`, `ddbedm_vocab`, `mocho_vocab` | ~0 — all from emitter Counters and dict lookups |
| `full` | same as `dispatch` (reserved for future additions) | same |

**Recommendation for full-corpus runs**: `--stats dispatch`. All predicate and class counts are collected during emission — no post-hoc N-Quad scanning.

---

## Schema

---

## Field rationale

### `run`
Reproducibility: timestamp links the stats file to the run directory; input path confirms which corpus was used; `stats_level` records which sections are present.

### `records`
Pipeline accounting. Sum invariant:
```
processed = dispatch.htype_hits + dispatch.mediatype_hits + dispatch.fallback_d9 + skipped_mt007
```
`skipped_not_in_ids` counts records present in the JSONL but absent from the ID filter file.

`by_mediatype` — mediatype distribution within the run (short codes: `mt001`–`mt007`). Since each production run is per-sector, this shows the content-type mix for that sector. Values sum to `records.processed`.

`by_htype` — hierarchy type distribution (short codes: `ht021`, `ht030`, etc.). Empty for sectors with no htype (sparte005, sparte006). Values sum to records that carry a `hierarchyType` field.

### `triples.by_graph`
Validates the four-stream model: a graph at 0 triples signals a broken stream. `prov` carries ~1 triple per record; `ddbedm` is the largest stream (verbatim EDM passthrough). `werk` triples go to DuckDB, not to the N-Quads file.

### `ddbedm_classes`
Entity class instance counts in the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_EDM_ENTITY_TYPES` during emission, no N-Quad scanning. `edm:ProvidedCHO` count equals `records.processed` (every record has exactly one). Other classes vary: `edm:WebResource` reflects digitised items, `edm:Agent` reflects named persons/organisations.

Used in the paper to characterise ddbedm graph structure per sector.

### `ddbedm_vocab`
Predicate counts for the ddbedm passthrough graph — derived from `_DDBEDM_PROP` during emission. `properties_all` covers every predicate used across all entity types.

### `dispatch`
Core validation of §1.1 class assignment.

- `htype_hits` — records where htype drove primary class (htype-first strata)
- `mediatype_hits` — records where mediatype lookup drove primary class
- `fallback_d9` — records that received `mocho:Manifestation` because no specific class resolved (D9); lower is better
- `work_classes`, `expression_classes`, `manifestation_classes`, `rico_classes` — per-class counts by WEMI slot; `expression_classes` is currently empty (no E-level dispatch implemented)

### `mocho_vocab`
Predicate counts for the mocho named graph — derived from emitter Counters during emission, no post-hoc N-Quad regex.

- `properties_all` — every predicate used in the mocho stream, including passthrough EDM/DC properties; ordered by frequency
- `properties_new` — subset of `properties_all` restricted to namespaces introduced by the mocho alignment (RDA, RiCO, mocho, VRA, MO, ACO, EBUCore, DoCO); confirms that alignment predicates are exercised

New namespace prefixes counted under `properties_new`:
`rdam`, `rdaw`, `rdae`, `rdac`, `rico`, `ric-rst`, `mocho`, `vra`, `mo`, `aco`, `ec`, `doco`

### `werk_staging`
GND Werk linking staging table. `by_class` confirms only W-slot classes (`rdac:C10001`, `mo:MusicalWork`) produce staging rows, consistent with D26.

---

## Resource paper relevance

| Stat | Paper use |
|---|---|
| `records.processed`, `triples.total`, `triples.by_graph` | Dataset scale — corpus size and graph model coverage |
| `records.by_mediatype`, `records.by_htype` | Content characterisation per sector |
| `ddbedm_classes` | ddbedm graph structural completeness per sector |
| `dispatch.work_classes`, `dispatch.manifestation_classes`, `dispatch.rico_classes` | Ontology coverage — which WEMI levels and vocabularies are populated |
| `dispatch.fallback_d9` / `records.processed` | Alignment precision — share of corpus receiving specific typing vs. generic fallback |
| `mocho_vocab.properties_new` | New vocabulary in use — mocho/RDA properties are exercised, not just defined |
| `werk_staging.rows`, `werk_staging.by_class` | W-level entity resolution — evidence for GND Werk linking pipeline |

file.content:
def _escape_literal(s: str) -> str:
    """Escape backslash and double-quote for N-Triples/N-Quads literal content."""
    return s.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"')


def value_to_nt_obj(val: object) -> list[str]:
    """Convert a JSONL field value to a list of N-Triples object strings.

    Handles all value shapes produced by the DDB EDM JSONL:
      None / ""                    → []
      str (non-empty)              → ['"escaped"']
      list                         → recurse and flatten
      {"resource": IRI}            → ["<IRI>"]
      {"lang": L, "$": T}          → ['"T"@L']
      {"lang": null, "$": T}       → ['"T"']
      {"resource": null, "$": ""}  → []
    """
    if val is None:
        return []
    if isinstance(val, str):
        return [f'"{_escape_literal(val)}"'] if val else []
    if isinstance(val, list):
        result = []
        for item in val:
            result.extend(value_to_nt_obj(item))
        return result
    if isinstance(val, dict):
        resource = val.get("resource")
        if resource:
            return [f"<{resource}>"]
        text = val.get("$", "")
        if not text:
            return []
        escaped = _escape_literal(str(text))
        lang = val.get("lang")
        if lang:
            return [f'"{escaped}"@{lang}']
        return [f'"{escaped}"']
    return []


def normalize_date(s: str) -> list[str]:
    """Normalise a dc:date string to ISO 8601.

    8-digit compact YYYYMMDD → YYYY-MM-DD.
    ISO interval begin/end → [begin, end].
    All other values returned unchanged.
    """
    s = s.strip()
    if "/" in s:

1	"""Utility functions: N-Quads formatting, URI minting, value normalisation."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from .constants import (
6	    AgentDict, NQuad, NQList,
7	    GEMEA_BASE, DDB_ITEM_BASE,
8	    _PREFIXES, _MEDIATYPE_PREFIX, _SECTOR_PREFIX,
9	)
10	
11	
12	def make_nq(s_nt: str, p_nt: str, o_nt: str, graph_iri: str) -> NQuad:
13	    """Return one N-Quads line."""
14	    return f"{s_nt} {p_nt} {o_nt} <{graph_iri}> ."
15	
16	
17	def coerce_list(val: object) -> list:
18	    """Normalise None/dict/list to a list."""
19	    if val is None:
20	        return []
21	    if isinstance(val, dict):
22	        return [val]
23	    if isinstance(val, list):
24	        return val
25	    return []
26	
27	
28	def _expand_prefix(curie: str) -> str:
29	    """Expand a CURIE (e.g. 'rdam:P30134') to a full IRI."""
30	    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
31	        if curie.startswith(prefix + ":"):
32	            return base + curie[len(prefix) + 1:]
33	    return curie
34	
35	
36	def _to_curie(iri: str) -> str:
37	    """Collapse a full IRI to a CURIE using _PREFIXES; return IRI unchanged if no match."""
38	    for prefix, base in _PREFIXES.items():
39	        if iri.startswith(base):
40	            return f"{prefix}:{iri[len(base):]}"
41	    return iri
42	
43	
44	def mint_cho_uri(obj_id: str) -> str:
45	    """Return the minted GeMeA mocho CHO URI for a 32-char DDB object ID (D22)."""
46	    return GEMEA_BASE + obj_id
47	
48	
49	def mint_bare_id(entity_class: str, raw_id: str) -> str:
50	    """Expand a bare 32-char ID to a full URI (D27).
51	
52	    ProvidedCHO bare IDs → DDB item URI.
53	    All other entity types → urn:ddbedm:<ClassName>:<id>.
54	    Full URIs and URNs are returned unchanged.
55	    """
56	    if raw_id.startswith(("http", "urn")):
57	        return raw_id
58	    if entity_class == "ProvidedCHO":
59	        return DDB_ITEM_BASE + raw_id
60	    return f"urn:ddbedm:{entity_class}:{raw_id}"
61	
62

content.content:
268	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
269	
270	# Characters forbidden inside N-Triples IRI references (RFC 3987 + NT spec)
271	_IRI_UNSAFE_RE = re.compile(r'[\x00-\x20<>"{}|\\^`\x7f]')
272	
273	
274	def _sanitize_iri(iri: str) -> str:
275	    """Percent-encode characters that are illegal inside NT IRI references."""
276	    return _IRI_UNSAFE_RE.sub(lambda m: f"%{ord(m.group()):02X}", iri)
277	
278	
279	def to_named_node(val: str, entity_type: str) -> px.NamedNode | None:
280	    """Mint a NamedNode for a URI or bare DDB ID.
281	
282	    ProvidedCHO bare IDs → http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/item/<id>
283	    All other entity types → urn:ddbedm:<ClassName>:<id>
284	    """
285	    if not val or not isinstance(val, str):
286	        return None
287	    if val.startswith("http") or val.startswith("urn"):
288	        try:
289	            return px.NamedNode(_sanitize_iri(val))
290	        except Exception:
291	            return None
292	    if entity_type == "ProvidedCHO":
293	        return px.NamedNode(DDB_ITEM + val)
294	    try:
295	        return px.NamedNode(_sanitize_iri(DDBEDM + entity_type + ":" + val))
296	    except Exception:
297	        return None

# GeMeA — Claude Project Instructions

German Memory Atlas: KG browser for 65M DDB cultural heritage objects + ISWC 2026 Resource Track paper.

## Start here
Read `.claude/gemea_pipeline.md` first. It has the current phase order, script locations, directory layout, external services, tech stack, and key notes. The skills below are indexed against it.

## Available role skills

Invoke these to set the working lens for a session:

| Skill | When to use |
|-------|------------|
| `/expert_bash_scripter` | Writing or reviewing shell scripts in `scripts/sh/` |
| `/senior_python_developer` | Writing or reviewing Python in `scripts/py/`, `ingest/`, or `api/` |
| `/senior_ux_developer` | Working on `frontend/` |
| `/senior_researcher` | NLP experiments, evaluation design, ontology/KG decisions, DH analysis, paper writing |

## QLever / MCP queries

Before executing any SPARQL query or MCP tool call against QLever:
1. Write the query to `notes/mcp-server-qlever/sparql-exploration.md` under a descriptive `##` heading.
2. Only then execute it (via MCP tool or `curl http://localhost:7020`).

This creates a running log of all exploration queries for reproducibility and the paper.

## Conventions
- Before exploring the codebase or planning a new script, ask clarifying questions first: purpose, inputs (format, schema, location), outputs (format, location), constraints, and what already exists.
- Shell scripts → `scripts/sh/`; Python scripts → `scripts/py/`; pipeline modules → `ingest/`
- Every script has the standard header block (purpose, usage, inputs/outputs, dependencies, assumptions)
- Notes → `notes/`; per-SR decision records → `notes/ner/`; paper → `paper/`; LaTeX source → `paper/latex/`
- Intra-project links to notes, data, and scripts use full GitHub paths: `https://github.com/anntanp/gemea/blob/main/<path>`
- Data inputs → `data/raw/`; processed outputs → `data/processed/`
- PNG figures → `notes/images/`
- After saving any PNG, add an entry to `notes/images/README.md`: filename, title, one-sentence description, producing script
- Any question requiring a number ("how many", "how much", "what fraction", "what percentage", etc.) must be answered by first writing a script, running it, and saving the raw output to a CSV in `data/processed/` — never by computing inline or answering from memory
- No hardcoded secrets; no hardcoded absolute paths
- Tests alongside code; `mypy --strict` + `ruff` clean before committing Python

## DDB Sector (sparte) and Mediatype reference

**Sector (sparte)**

| Code | Sector |
|---|---|
| sparte001 | Archive |
| sparte002 | Library |
| sparte003 | Monument Preservation |
| sparte004 | Research |
| sparte005 | Media Library |
| sparte006 | Museum |
| sparte007 | Others |

**Mediatype (mt)**

| Code | Mediatype |
|---|---|
| mt001 | Audio |
| mt002 | Photo |
| mt003 | Text |
| mt005 | Video |
| mt007 | Not Digitized |

## Paper deadline
Abstract: 2 May 2026 — Full paper: 7 May 2026 — Camera-ready: 6 Aug 2026

Now I understand. The `_sanitize_iri` function in `export_ddb.py` percent-encodes characters that are illegal inside N-Triples IRI references. This sanitisation is applied when URIs are created as `NamedNode` objects.

In the transform package, `value_to_nt_obj` in `utils.py` handles the conversion of values to N-Quads object strings. When it gets a `{"resource": IRI}` dict, it returns `[f"<{resource}>"]` without any sanitisation. Similarly, `make_nq` just puts whatever IRI it gets directly into `<...>`.


So I need to port the sanitisation function over to the transform package and apply it in `value_to_nt_obj` when handling resource IRIs. I should also add a counter to track how many IRIs required sanitisation during each run, which would be a new stat tracked in the main module.

The tricky part is the design for counting — I could have `_sanitize_iri` return a tuple with both the sanitised IRI and a flag indicating whether it was changed, then thread that information through the call chain. Or I could use a module-level counter, though that feels less clean. Let me explore a few options here... counter)` that increments the counter if sanitisation was needed.

Actually the simplest approach might be:
- Add `_sanitize_iri` to `utils.py`  
- Apply it in `value_to_nt_obj` when handling `{"resource": ...}` case
- For counting: check if the sanitised IRI differs from the original, and if so, increment a counter

But `value_to_nt_obj` doesn't have access to a counter... 

Let me think differently. The sanitisation should happen at the `value_to_nt_obj` level (or at least at the `make_nq` level), and for counting, the simplest approach is to have `value_to_nt_obj` return both the NQ objects AND a count of sanitisations performed. But this changes the interface.

Or I could separate concerns — have a function that returns the IRI and whether it was sanitised, letting callers accumulate counts themselves. Looking at the actual flow, `value_to_nt_obj` returns N-Quads strings and gets called throughout the emitters, so the cleanest solution is to apply sanitisation silently inside it while using a module-level counter in `utils.py` that gets incremented each time, then export a function to read and reset it. Though module-level mutable state feels messy, the most practical approach for this is probably to have `value_to_nt_obj` return both the sanitised N-Quads and a count of how many sanitisations occurred.

The real question is where sanitisation actually happens in the pipeline — it needs to occur in `value_to_nt_obj` for the resource case, but also in various places in `emitters.py` where URIs are directly formatted into N-Quads strings, and potentially in the minting logic too.

I think the cleanest solution is to create a dedicated sanitisation function that can track counts as a side effect, maybe by accepting an optional counter dictionary that gets updated whenever sanitisation actually occurs.

Defining the sanitization function... I'm thinking through the simplest approach: move the sanitization logic to `utils.py`, apply it in `value_to_nt_obj` when handling IRIs, have that function return both the NT strings and a count of sanitized IRIs, then thread that count through all the callers in `emitters.py` so the emission functions can report back the total sanitization count.

But updating `value_to_nt_obj`'s return type everywhere it's called—probably 20+ places—is a lot of churn. I'm considering a cleaner alternative using a counter that gets passed through the call chain rather than relying on module-level state. I'll pass the counter through the recursive calls in `value_to_nt_obj`, then in both `emit_ddbedm_triples` and `emit_mocho_triples` I'll instantiate a local counter, pass it through, and return the sanitization counts alongside the triples.

Then in `transform.py` I'll unpack those counts from the emitters and add them to the appropriate info dictionaries, while `__main__.py` accumulates the total `uri_sanitized` count for the stats output.

But I'm realizing `value_to_nt_obj` gets called in other places too where we might not want to track this...

Actually, looking at the direct IRI emissions in the emitters where we extract the URI from the `value_to_nt_obj` result and reuse it, those would already be counted since they came from that function in the first place.

But I need to check the cases where URIs come straight from the source data without going through `value_to_nt_obj` first - like in `emit_aggregation_triples` where we're pulling `obj_nt` directly. The user wants sanitisation applied throughout the pipeline and counted, so I should review the full scope of where this needs to happen in `emitters.py` and apply `_sanitize_iri` consistently at the right entry points. Since all URIs flow through `value_to_nt_obj` first, I only need to sanitize there—any later URI extraction will already have clean values. """Triple emitters: ddbedm passthrough, mocho alignment, PROV-O, and werk_staging."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter

from .constants import (
    AgentDict, NQList, PropAlign,
    RDF_TYPE, RDFS_LABEL, OWL_SAMEAS,
    DCTERMS_SOURCE, DCTERMS_ID, DCTERMS_TYPE, DCTERMS_HAS_VER, DCTERMS_REF,
    DCTERMS_RIGHTS, DCTERMS_CREATOR, DCTERMS_SUBJECT,
    DC_ID, DC_TITLE, DC_DESCRIPTION, DC_SUBJECT, DC_CONTRIBUTOR,
    FOAF_ORG, FOAF_NAME, FOAF_THUMBNAIL,
    SCHEMA_URL, MOCHO_ISIL, MOCHO_NS, MOCHO_AGENT, RICO_HAS_RST,
    PROV_ENTITY, PROV_AGENT, PROV_SW_AGENT,
    PROV_DERIVED, PROV_ATTRIBUTED, PROV_GEN_TIME, PROV_ON_BEHALF,
    DCAT_DATASET, XSD_DATETIME,
    DDB_BASE, DDB_ITEM_BASE, DDB_API_BASE, EDM_NS,
    _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES, _DDBEDM_PROP, _MOCHO_SKIP, _NEW_NS,
    _CLASS_WEMI, _CONTRIBUTOR_COL, _W_SLOT_CLASSES, SUBJECT_KEYS,
)
from .utils import (
    make_nq, coerce_list, mint_bare_id, _escape_literal,
    value_to_nt_obj, normalize_date, is_ddb_or_gnd, resolve_agent, _to_curie,
)


def emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf: dict, graph_iri: str) -> tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter]:
    """Emit verbatim EDM passthrough triples for all entity types in rdf (§6.1).

    Subject: original entity['about'] URI. Includes mt007 records.
    Returns (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr) — counters use CURIEs, built during emission.
    """
    lines:     NQList  = []
    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()

"""Utility functions: N-Quads formatting, URI minting, value normalisation."""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from collections import Counter

from .constants import (
    AgentDict, NQuad, NQList,
    GEMEA_BASE, DDB_ITEM_BASE,
    _PREFIXES, _MEDIATYPE_PREFIX, _SECTOR_PREFIX,
)

# Characters forbidden inside N-Triples IRI references (RFC 3987 + NT spec)
_IRI_UNSAFE_RE = re.compile(r'[\x00-\x20<>"{}|\\^`\x7f]')


def _sanitize_iri(iri: str) -> str:
    """Percent-encode characters illegal inside NT IRI references."""
    return _IRI_UNSAFE_RE.sub(lambda m: f"%{ord(m.group()):02X}", iri)

def value_to_nt_obj(val: object) -> list[str]:
    """Convert a JSONL field value to a list of N-Triples object strings.

    Handles all value shapes produced by the DDB EDM JSONL:
      None / ""                    → []
      str (non-empty)              → ['"escaped"']
      list                         → recurse and flatten
      {"resource": IRI}            → ["<IRI>"]
      {"lang": L, "$": T}          → ['"T"@L']
      {"lang": null, "$": T}       → ['"T"']
      {"resource": null, "$": ""}  → []
    """
    if val is None:
        return []
    if isinstance(val, str):
        return [f'"{_escape_literal(val)}"'] if val else []
    if isinstance(val, list):
        result = []
        for item in val:
            result.extend(value_to_nt_obj(item))
        return result
    if isinstance(val, dict):
        resource = val.get("resource")
        if resource:
            return [f"<{resource}>"]
        text = val.get("$", "")
        if not text:
            return []
        escaped = _escape_literal(str(text))
        lang = val.get("lang")
        if lang:
            return [f'"{escaped}"@{lang}']
        return [f'"{escaped}"']
    return []
input.new_string:
def value_to_nt_obj(val: object, sani_ctr: Counter | None = None) -> list[str]:
    """Convert a JSONL field value to a list of N-Triples object strings.

    Handles all value shapes produced by the DDB EDM JSONL:
      None / ""                    → []
      str (non-empty)              → ['"escaped"']
      list                         → recurse and flatten
      {"resource": IRI}            → ["<IRI>"] (IRI percent-encoded if unsafe chars present)
      {"lang": L, "$": T}          → ['"T"@L']
      {"lang": null, "$": T}       → ['"T"']
      {"resource": null, "$": ""}  → []

    sani_ctr: if provided, incremented once per IRI that required sanitisation.
    """
    if val is None:
        return []
    if isinstance(val, str):
        return [f'"{_escape_literal(val)}"'] if val else []
    if isinstance(val, list):
        result = []
        for item in val:
            result.extend(value_to_nt_obj(item, sani_ctr))
        return result
    if isinstance(val, dict):
        resource = val.get("resource")
        if resource:
            sanitized = _sanitize_iri(resource)
            if sani_ctr is not None and sanitized != resource:
                sani_ctr["uri_sanitized"] += 1
            return [f"<{sanitized}>"]
        text = val.get("$", "")
        if not text:
            return []
        escaped = _escape_literal(str(text))
        lang = val.get("lang")
        if lang:
            return [f'"{escaped}"@{lang}']
        return [f'"{escaped}"']
    return []

def emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf: dict, graph_iri: str) -> tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter]:
    """Emit verbatim EDM passthrough triples for all entity types in rdf (§6.1).

    Subject: original entity['about'] URI. Includes mt007 records.
    Returns (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr) — counters use CURIEs, built during emission.
    """
    lines:     NQList  = []
    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()
    pred_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    _skip = frozenset({"about"})
    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
        edm_class = _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES.get(entity_type)
        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
                continue
            raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
            if not raw_about:
                continue
            subj_uri = mint_bare_id(entity_type, raw_about)
            subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
            if edm_class:
                lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{edm_class}>", graph_iri))
                class_ctr[_to_curie(edm_class)] += 1
            for key, val in entity.items():
                if key in _skip:
                    continue
                pred_iri = _DDBEDM_PROP.get(key)
                if not pred_iri:
                    continue
                pred_nt = f"<{pred_iri}>"
                curie   = _to_curie(pred_iri)
                for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(val):
                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, pred_nt, obj_nt, graph_iri))
                    pred_ctr[curie] += 1
    return lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr

    """Emit verbatim EDM passthrough triples for all entity types in rdf (§6.1).

    Subject: original entity['about'] URI. Includes mt007 records.
    Returns (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, uri_sanitized) — counters use CURIEs, built during emission.
    """
    lines:     NQList  = []
    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()
    pred_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    sani_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    _skip = frozenset({"about"})
    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
        edm_class = _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES.get(entity_type)
        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
                continue
            raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
            if not raw_about:
                continue
            subj_uri = mint_bare_id(entity_type, raw_about)
            subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
            if edm_class:
                lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{edm_class}>", graph_iri))
                class_ctr[_to_curie(edm_class)] += 1
            for key, val in entity.items():
                if key in _skip:
                    continue
                pred_iri = _DDBEDM_PROP.get(key)
                if not pred_iri:
                    continue
                pred_nt = f"<{pred_iri}>"
                curie   = _to_curie(pred_iri)
                for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(val, sani_ctr):
                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, pred_nt, obj_nt, graph_iri))
                    pred_ctr[curie] += 1
    return lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr["uri_sanitized"]

def emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf: dict, graph_iri: str) -> tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter, int]:
    """Emit verbatim EDM passthrough triples for all entity types in rdf (§6.1).

    Subject: original entity['about'] URI. Includes mt007 records.
    Returns (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, uri_sanitized) — counters use CURIEs, built during emission.
    """
    lines:     NQList  = []
    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()
    pred_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    sani_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    _skip = frozenset({"about"})
    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
        edm_class = _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES.get(entity_type)
        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
                continue
            raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
            if not raw_about:
                continue
            subj_uri = mint_bare_id(entity_type, raw_about)
            subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
            if edm_class:
                lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{edm_class}>", graph_iri))
                class_ctr[_to_curie(edm_class)] += 1
            for key, val in entity.items():
                if key in _skip:
                    continue
                pred_iri = _DDBEDM_PROP.get(key)
                if not pred_iri:
                    continue
                pred_nt = f"<{pred_iri}>"
                curie   = _to_curie(pred_iri)
                for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(val, sani_ctr):
                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, pred_nt, obj_nt, graph_iri))
                    pred_ctr[curie] += 1
    return lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr["uri_sanitized"]

content.thinking:
Now I understand the problem. The user is seeing a warning: `IRI ref not standard-compliant: <http://www.geonames.org/2856883 http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7005252>`

This means that in the raw DDB data, the `edm:currentLocation` (or similar) field has **two URIs concatenated with a space** in a single `resource` value: `http://www.geonames.org/2856883 http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7005252`. 

The `value_to_nt_obj` function takes a `{"resource": "http://www.geonames.org/2856883 http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7005252"}` dict and produces `<http://www.geonames.org/2856883 http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7005252>` as a single IRI reference —

which is invalid because spaces aren't allowed in IRIs. The sanitization function would catch the space and percent-encode it, but that still leaves us with a malformed single URI instead of two separate ones. The real solution is to detect when a resource field contains multiple space-separated URIs and split them into individual triples, since this is fundamentally a data quality issue in the source.

content.text:

The fix is to split on whitespace in `value_to_nt_obj` when handling `resource` values — multiple URIs become multiple NT strings, each getting sanitised independently. While we're there, this also gives us the right place for the `_sanitize_iri` call.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Core transform: orchestrates per-record triple emission."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter

from .constants import (
    NQList, PropAlign,
    MT007_IRI, GRAPH_DDBEDM, GRAPH_MOCHO, GRAPH_PROV,
)
from .utils import get_object_id, mint_bare_id, mint_cho_uri, _extract_mediatype_sector, coerce_list
from .emitters import emit_ddbedm_triples, emit_prov_triples, emit_mocho_triples, werk_staging_row


def transform_record(
    record: dict,
    ids_set: set[str] | None,
    mediatype_class_map: dict,
    htype_map: dict,
    audio_type2class: dict,
    class_prop_align: PropAlign,
    lido_dispatch: dict,
) -> tuple[dict[str, NQList], dict | None, dict, dict]:
    """Transform one JSONL record into per-graph N-Quads lists (§7.1).

    Returns (streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info).
    streams is empty dict when record is filtered by IDs.
    dispatch_info: {"target_class", "wemi", "htype_used", "fallback", "is_mt007", "mediatype"}
    pred_info: {"ddbedm_classes", "ddbedm_preds", "mocho_preds_all", "mocho_preds_new"} — Counters
    """
    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
    if obj_id is None:
        raise ValueError("Cannot extract object ID from record")

    if ids_set is not None and obj_id not in ids_set:
        return {}, None, {}, {}

    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
    cho: dict = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
    if isinstance(cho, list):
        cho = cho[0] if cho else {}

    ddb_uri = mint_bare_id("ProvidedCHO", (cho.get("about") or obj_id).strip())
    cho_uri = mint_cho_uri(obj_id)

    mediatype, sector = _extract_mediatype_sector(rdf.get("Concept"))
    is_mt007 = (mediatype == MT007_IRI)

    streams: dict[str, NQList] = {}

    # Streams [1] and [4] always run, including mt007 (faithfulness + audit trail)
    ddbedm_lines, ddbedm_classes, ddbedm_preds = emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf, GRAPH_DDBEDM)
    streams["ddbedm"] = ddbedm_lines
    streams["prov"]   = emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_uri, GRAPH_PROV)

    # Stream [2] mocho and [3] werk: skip mt007 (D15)
    werk_row: dict | None = None
    dispatch_info: dict = {"target_class": "", "wemi": "", "htype_used": False,
                           "fallback": False, "is_mt007": is_mt007, "mediatype": mediatype}
    if not is_mt007:
        mocho_lines, target_class, wemi, dflags = emit_mocho_triples(
            rdf, cho_uri, ddb_uri, sector, mediatype,
            mediatype_class_map, htype_map, audio_type2class,
            class_prop_align, lido_dispatch, GRAPH_MOCHO,
        )
        streams["mocho"] = mocho_lines
        werk_row = werk_staging_row(cho_uri, cho, target_class)
        mocho_preds_all = dflags.pop("preds_all", Counter())
        mocho_preds_new = dflags.pop("preds_new", Counter())
        dispatch_info.update({"target_class": target_class, "wemi": wemi, **dflags})
    else:
        mocho_preds_all = Counter()
        mocho_preds_new = Counter()

    pred_info: dict = {
        "ddbedm_classes":  ddbedm_classes,
        "ddbedm_preds":    ddbedm_preds,
        "mocho_preds_all": mocho_preds_all,
        "mocho_preds_new": mocho_preds_new,
    }
    return streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info

"""Core transform: orchestrates per-record triple emission."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter

from .constants import (
    NQList, PropAlign,
    MT007_IRI, GRAPH_DDBEDM, GRAPH_MOCHO, GRAPH_PROV,
)
from .utils import get_object_id, mint_bare_id, mint_cho_uri, _extract_mediatype_sector, coerce_list
from .emitters import emit_ddbedm_triples, emit_prov_triples, emit_mocho_triples, werk_staging_row


def transform_record(
    record: dict,
    ids_set: set[str] | None,
    mediatype_class_map: dict,
    htype_map: dict,
    audio_type2class: dict,
    class_prop_align: PropAlign,
    lido_dispatch: dict,
) -> tuple[dict[str, NQList], dict | None, dict, dict]:
    """Transform one JSONL record into per-graph N-Quads lists (§7.1).

    Returns (streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info).
    streams is empty dict when record is filtered by IDs.
    dispatch_info: {"target_class", "wemi", "htype_used", "fallback", "is_mt007", "mediatype"}
    pred_info: {"ddbedm_classes", "ddbedm_preds", "mocho_preds_all", "mocho_preds_new"} — Counters
    """
    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
    if obj_id is None:
        raise ValueError("Cannot extract object ID from record")

    if ids_set is not None and obj_id not in ids_set:
        return {}, None, {}, {}

    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
    cho: dict = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
    if isinstance(cho, list):
        cho = cho[0] if cho else {}

    ddb_uri = mint_bare_id("ProvidedCHO", (cho.get("about") or obj_id).strip())
    cho_uri = mint_cho_uri(obj_id)

    mediatype, sector = _extract_mediatype_sector(rdf.get("Concept"))
    is_mt007 = (mediatype == MT007_IRI)

    streams: dict[str, NQList] = {}

    # Streams [1] and [4] always run, including mt007 (faithfulness + audit trail)
    ddbedm_lines, ddbedm_classes, ddbedm_preds, ddbedm_sani = emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf, GRAPH_DDBEDM)
    streams["ddbedm"] = ddbedm_lines
    streams["prov"]   = emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_uri, GRAPH_PROV)

    # Stream [2] mocho and [3] werk: skip mt007 (D15)
    werk_row: dict | None = None
    dispatch_info: dict = {"target_class": "", "wemi": "", "htype_used": False,
                           "fallback": False, "is_mt007": is_mt007, "mediatype": mediatype}
    if not is_mt007:
        mocho_lines, target_class, wemi, dflags = emit_mocho_triples(
            rdf, cho_uri, ddb_uri, sector, mediatype,
            mediatype_class_map, htype_map, audio_type2class,
            class_prop_align, lido_dispatch, GRAPH_MOCHO,
        )
        streams["mocho"] = mocho_lines
        werk_row = werk_staging_row(cho_uri, cho, target_class)
        mocho_preds_all = dflags.pop("preds_all", Counter())
        mocho_preds_new = dflags.pop("preds_new", Counter())
        dispatch_info.update({"target_class": target_class, "wemi": wemi, **dflags})
    else:
        mocho_preds_all = Counter()
        mocho_preds_new = Counter()

    pred_info: dict = {
        "ddbedm_classes":  ddbedm_classes,
        "ddbedm_preds":    ddbedm_preds,
        "mocho_preds_all": mocho_preds_all,
        "mocho_preds_new": mocho_preds_new,
    }
    return streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info

content.text:
All working. Summary of what was done and the numbers:

**Root cause**: 1,250 `resource` fields in the DDB data (across `currentLocation`, `isShownAt`, `isShownBy`, `object`, subject fields, `creator`, `contributor`, `happenedAt`) contain multiple space-separated URIs concatenated into one string. `value_to_nt_obj` was emitting the whole string as a single IRI reference, producing invalid N-Quads.

**Fix**: `value_to_nt_obj` now splits `resource` on whitespace and emits each URI as a separate NT object string, with `_sanitize_iri` applied to each one (ported from `export_ddb.py`).

**Corpus counts (goethe-faust full run)**:
- `uri_split: 1503` — individual URIs extracted from multi-URI values (1,250 fields → 1,503 URIs, so some fields had 3+ URIs)
- `uri_sanitized: 29` — IRIs that needed percent-encoding of illegal characters

Both are now reported under `records` in `transform_stats.json` at all stats levels.

The user is asking about the `about` field in entities - when an entity has multiple URIs in its `about` field. Let me check how `about` is handled in the code.

In `emit_ddbedm_triples`, the code does:
```python
raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
if not raw_about:
    continue

subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
```

So `about` is used as the subject URI. 

If `about` contains multiple space-separated URIs, the whole string gets passed to `mint_bare_id` which would likely create a malformed URI rather than handling each one separately. I need to check whether the actual data even has multiple URIs in a single `about` field.

content.text: Good question — `about` is used as the **subject** URI, not an object, so `value_to_nt_obj` never touches it. Let me check whether `about` fields also have the same multi-URI problem:

input.description: Check if about fields contain multiple space-separated URIs

content.thinking:
Yes, `about` fields also have multiple space-separated URIs. These are used as the subject URI in `emit_ddbedm_triples`. Currently:

```python
raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
if not raw_about:
    continue
subj_uri = mint_bare_id(entity_type, raw_about)
subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
```

`mint_bare_id` passes the full string to create a subject URI, which would include spaces if the `about` field has multiple URIs. This creates an invalid subject URI.


For multiple `about` URIs, I should treat them as `owl:sameAs` relationships—pick the first one as the canonical subject and emit `owl:sameAs` triples linking it to the others. Since this is in the ddbedm passthrough graph, this approach preserves the original semantics while maintaining a valid RDF structure.

For the mocho graph, the situation is different: the mocho transform generates its own CHO URI using a minting function rather than relying on `about` as the subject. Instead, the `about` value gets processed through a bare ID minting function to create a ddb_uri that's then used for the `owl:sameAs` relationship.

The fix needs to handle both cases: split the `about` field on whitespace in the ddbedm passthrough, using the first URI as the subject and emitting `owl:sameAs` triples for any additional ones, while also ensuring the bare ID minting function in the mocho transform only processes the first URI from `about`.

content.text:
Yes — same problem, but worse: multi-URI `about` becomes the **subject** IRI, creating an invalid subject node for every triple emitted for that entity. 1,178 entities affected (761 `Place`, 386 `WebResource`, 31 `Agent`).

Currently `emit_ddbedm_triples` does:
```python
raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
subj_uri  = mint_bare_id(entity_type, raw_about)   # full "uri1 uri2" string → broken subject
```

The right fix: split, use the first URI as the subject, emit `owl:sameAs` for the remainder. That's the natural RDF interpretation — the `about` URIs all identify the same entity.

There's also a second occurrence in `transform.py` where `cho.get("about")` feeds `mint_bare_id` for the ddbedm passthrough subject:
```python
ddb_uri = mint_bare_id("ProvidedCHO", (cho.get("about") or obj_id).strip())
```

Shall I fix both, counting the extra `owl:sameAs` triples emitted as a new `uri_about_split` stat?

The user wants me to fix both `emit_ddbedm_triples` and `transform.py` for multi-URI `about` fields, and count the extra `owl:sameAs` triples emitted.

1. **`utils.py`**: Add a helper `_split_about(raw_about: str) -> list[str]` that splits an `about` value into a list of sanitized URIs. Or I can just use `raw_about.split()` inline.

2. **`emitters.py` — `emit_ddbedm_triples`**:
   - Split `raw_about` on whitespace and sanitize each URI
   - Use the first one as the subject for the main triples
   - Emit `owl:sameAs` links from the primary URI to any additional ones
   - Track when we split an `about` field with the counter

3. **`transform.py`**:
   - When minting the DDB URI, extract just the first URI from a potentially multi-URI `about` field
   - The extra URIs get handled via `owl:sameAs` emission in the emitter, so I don't need to do anything special there

4. **`__main__.py`**: Wire up the `uri_about_split` counter to the stats output

I need to figure out how to thread the `uri_about_split` count back through the return values. Since `emit_ddbedm_triples` already returns multiple values including sanitization counts, I should extend that tuple to include this new counter...

Actually, the cleanest approach is to return the entire sanitization counter object from `emit_ddbedm_triples` instead of just extracting a single value. That way both `uri_sanitized` and `uri_about_split` are available in `transform.py` without changing the function signature too much. I'll update the return type to include the full Counter object.

For `emit_mocho_triples`, I don't need to worry about the `about_split` handling since that field is only relevant for the ddbedm graph. The mocho graph uses its own URI minting logic. However, I do need to fix how `transform.py` extracts the URI from the `about` field—it should only take the first URI when splitting occurs.

Now for `emitters.py`, I'm implementing the logic to split the `about` field on whitespace, sanitize each part, use the first as the primary subject URI, and create `owl:sameAs` links for any additional URIs while tracking the count.

Writing emitter code... I need to add `_sanitize_iri` to the imports from `utils.py` in `emitters.py`. Then I'll change the return type of `emit_ddbedm_triples` to return the full sanitization counter instead of just the count, so I can extract the specific metrics in `transform.py`.

Updating the unpacking in `transform.py` to capture the counter, then I'll add those sanitization metrics directly to the `pred_info` dictionary alongside the existing counters.

For the `ddb_uri` logic, I need to handle the case where `about` might be empty—if it falls back to `obj_id`, I shouldn't split that, so I'll check if `about_str` has content before splitting on whitespace.

Now I'm adding `uri_about_split` to the records output in `__main__.py` alongside the other URI metrics. For the counter itself, I'm tracking the number of extra URIs generated (the split count minus one), which corresponds directly to the additional `owl:sameAs` triples being emitted. Looking at the test data with multiple space-separated URIs, this approach makes sense—each extra URI becomes one more triple.
